metadata.title: Da Valenca Mechanica: Leonardo da Vinci's Codex Madrid I as a Modular Mechanical Language
metadata.description: This paper argues that Codex Madrid I is best understood not as a miscellaneous notebook of mechanical sketches, but as a structured corpus of reusable engineering modules whose coherence emerges when the manuscript is read simultaneously at three levels: text as demonstration, reverse folio sequence as instruction, and marginal note as governing law. Six original priority claims are registered, none of which appears in the prior scholarship: the complete flintlock prototype (c.1493–97, 115 years before le Bourgeoys); the resonant impulse accumulation principle as the theoretical basis of escapement physics; the combined clock-and-orrery modular platform identification; the Chiaravalle abbey clock as a precision dating anchor; seepage alignment as intentional design registration; and the over-centre toggle-lock doctrine.

creators.name: Vallance, Richard Steven
creators.affiliation: Da Valenca — Leonardo Project, United Kingdom

metadata.notes: Independent scholarly research. Primary sources: BNE Ms.8937 (Madrid Codex I), folios f.0r–f.25r, BNE transcript files 5869–5956. © 2026 Richard Steven Vallance. All Intellectual Property and Copyright Reserved.
